description: FastAPI REST API layer with all endpoints, comprehensive test suite with sample documents, Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, and README documentation. This makes the system accessible and deployable.
steps: Create api/main.py with FastAPI app: Configure CORS, include all route routers, add startup event to initialize database (audit logger init_db), configure OpenAPI docs with title 'Document Intelligence Agent API' and description. Add exception handlers for common errors. Mount static files if needed for document uploads (create uploads/ directory)

Create api/routes/health.py with: GET /health returns {status: 'healthy', version, uptime}, GET /metrics returns basic metrics (total_extractions, avg_processing_time, extractions_by_mode from audit log)

Create api/routes/extract.py with: POST /extract/local (upload file, extract with local mode), POST /extract/api (upload file, extract with API mode), POST /extract/hybrid (upload file, extract with hybrid mode), POST /extract/auto (upload file, auto-detect best mode based on document type). All endpoints accept file upload + optional config overrides in query params. Return ExtractionResult JSON. Include document classification in response

Create api/routes/batch.py with: POST /batch/process (accept multiple files + mode, start async batch job, return job_id), GET /batch/status/{job_id} (return progress), GET /batch/result/{job_id} (return results when complete), DELETE /batch/{job_id} (cancel batch job)

Create api/routes/actions.py with: GET /actions/rules (list configured action rules), POST /actions/rules (add new rule), PUT /actions/rules/{rule_id} (update rule), DELETE /actions/rules/{rule_id} (remove rule), POST /actions/evaluate (manually evaluate rules against a given extraction result)

Create api/routes/webhooks.py with: GET /webhooks (list registered webhooks), POST /webhooks (register new webhook with URL, events, secret), DELETE /webhooks/{webhook_id} (remove webhook), POST /webhooks/{webhook_id}/test (send test event to webhook)

Create tests/sample_docs/sample_contract.txt with realistic contract text containing: parties, effective date, termination clause, confidentiality clause, payment terms, governing law, indemnification - enough to test all contract analysis features

Create tests/test_local_extractor.py with pytest tests: test extraction from plain text, test entity recognition (dates, names, amounts), test confidence scoring, test handling of empty/minimal text. Use sample_contract.txt as fixture

Create tests/test_api_extractor.py with pytest tests: test prompt construction, test response parsing (mock the API calls), test chunking for long documents, test error handling for API failures. Use unittest.mock to avoid real API calls

Create tests/test_hybrid_extractor.py with pytest tests: test full hybrid flow with mocked components, test escalation logic (low confidence triggers API), test result merging, test that high-confidence results skip API. Mock both LocalExtractor and APIExtractor
Create tests/test_contract_analyzer.py with pytest tests: test clause extraction from sample contract, test risk scoring, test obligation detection, test expiry date tracking. Use sample_contract.txt

Create tests/test_batch_processor.py with pytest tests: test batch job creation, test progress tracking, test concurrent processing with small batch, test cancellation, test error handling for individual failures in batch

Create Dockerfile: Multi-stage build. Stage 1: install dependencies. Stage 2: runtime with Python 3.11-slim, install tesseract-ocr system package, copy code and requirements, install Python deps, expose port 8000, CMD uvicorn api.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
Create docker-compose.yml: Service for the main app (build from Dockerfile, ports 8000:8000, env_file .env, volumes for uploads), optional redis service for future caching

Create README.md: Project title, description, features list (3 extraction modes explained), architecture diagram (ASCII), quick start guide (Docker and local), API documentation summary with example curl commands for each endpoint, configuration guide (settings.yaml explanation), environment variables reference, testing instructions, project structure explanation, technology stack
acceptance_criteria: FastAPI app starts without errors and serves /health endpoint
All API routes are registered and accessible (visible in /docs)
POST /extract/local accepts a file upload and returns a structured response
Batch endpoint creates jobs and tracks progress
All pytest tests pass (minimum 15 tests across all test files)
Tests use mocking appropriately - no real API calls to OpenAI/Anthropic in tests
README provides clear setup and usage instructions
Sample contract document is realistic enough to exercise extraction features
verification: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/ai-new-2 && python -c "from api.main import app; print(f'Routes: {len(app.routes)}')"
Run: cd /projects/sandbox/ai-new-2 && python -c "import uvicorn; from api.main import app; print('FastAPI app loads OK')"
Run: cd /projects/sandbox/ai-new-2 && docker build -t doc-intelligence-agent:test . && echo 'Docker build OK'
findings: All 35 tests pass (8 models + 8 API extractor + 5 local extractor + 4 hybrid extractor + 5 contract analyzer + 5 batch processor). FastAPI app loads with 23 routes. Docker builds successfully with multi-stage build. The DocumentAgent import in batch_processor.py is local (inside _process_single) so tests must patch src.agent.orchestrator.DocumentAgent. BaseExtractor validates non-empty text, so empty doc tests should assert ValueError.
